clinical_validity.rationale: Clinical claims remain bounded, but local actor behavior, specialty review, and live dialogue safety are still unvalidated.

psychometric_defensibility.rationale: The plan rejects autonomous scoring claims, but validated score use, rater calibration, and trace-quality evidence remain open.

technical_feasibility.rationale: The API asset-job lane, local model smoke, local voice smoke, Blender bake, IWSDK sidecar, and Quest CDP gates improve feasibility, but immersive entry, live voice, and target-hardware proof remain blocked.

architecture_coherence.rationale: Internal capability jobs, API startup wiring, and ArchUnitTS boundaries align with the intended monorepo architecture, while benchmark-gate evidence IDs still need reconciliation.

security_privacy.rationale: Local-only defaults and internal endpoints help, but voice disclosure, retention, misuse controls, and production worker isolation are not complete.

ux_workflow_fit.rationale: The station UX has in-scene text, trace controls, and sidecar learning paths, but headset comfort, hand/controller affordances, locomotion, and readability need a worn-headset report.

cost_performance_efficiency.rationale: The team avoided paid APIs and recorded local performance, but local voice latency and missing Quest performance evidence keep efficiency uncertain.

open_source_sustainability.rationale: Pinned package posture, audit/license gates, Blender tooling caveats, and IWSDK isolation reduce open-source risk, though IWSDK reference metadata and peer-range issues remain.

market_gtm_strength.rationale: Demo credibility improves with local evidence and asset lanes, but design-partner, legal, validation, and headset-readiness materials still block stronger market claims.

evidence_discipline.rationale: The latest work records exact blockers, smoke reports, benchmark reports, and architecture tests, but debt-ID drift prevents a near-green evidence posture.

implementation_readiness.rationale: Development can proceed against API asset-job contracts and package boundaries, but the next implementation priorities are still evidence closure and ledger repair.

adversarial_robustness.rationale: The plan absorbs attacks on local AI halo effects, voice latency, Quest theater, IWSDK enthusiasm, asset-pipeline optimism, and ledger drift.

legal_regulatory_resilience.rationale: The plan guards high-stakes, endorsement, diagnostic, scoring, retention, and voice-disclosure claims, but counsel-ready external governance remains incomplete.

specialty_clinical_generalizability.rationale: The architecture remains station-general, but specialty breadth still depends on expert-reviewed case-bank expansion and actor behavior validation.

critical_risks.summary: Stakeholders may treat local model and voice smoke results as live clinical simulation readiness.
Quest shell, CDP frame, and sidecar evidence may still be confused with worn-headset immersive comfort evidence.
The asset-job facade may be mistaken for production asset-generation readiness before rigging, animation, texture, LOD, collider, and headset budget evidence exists.
IWSDK sidecar momentum may pull incompatible or oversized packages into the primary XR runtime before MCP, bundle, peer-range, and Quest evidence clears.
Evidence ledger ID drift may cause leadership to believe a blocker was closed or reopened for the wrong reason.

evidence_debt.summary: Capture and validate a human worn-headset Quest manual performance report that proves immersive entry, readable in-scene text, controller/hand affordances, locomotion comfort, frame pacing, heat, and battery observations.
Run structured-output, hidden-truth, actor-policy, and target M4 Pro or M4 Max local model benchmarks before enabling local dialogue in station runtime.
Prove streaming local voice with first audible playback latency, WebXR playback, disclosure, retention, and misuse controls before live voice use.
Align IWSDK benchmark-gate IDs with scorecard debt and record Phase 2 devtools, MCP inventory, Vite peer-range, reference metadata, bundle, and Quest evidence before runtime adoption.
Add production-grade asset-pipeline reports for generated humans, skin, clothing, rigging, animation, equipment, optimization, and headset budgets before calling the asset lane production-ready.
Prior local model, local voice, and Blender placeholder-bake installation smokes were captured without cloud spend and are sufficient to retire the narrower installation-only blockers.

decision_debt.summary: Decide whether benchmark gate IDs should follow the original debt item or the current capability area when evidence evolves across iterations.

summary: Iteration 0009 is amber: implementation readiness improves through API asset-job routing, architecture enforcement, and local evidence capture, but headset validation, live voice/dialogue, production assets, and evidence-ledger integrity remain blocking.
